frame_analyses.description: Setup showing FLS peg transfer board with 6 rubber pegs on right side of board, left side empty, all four graspers in ready position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard peg transfer setup, pegs positioned in correct quadrant

frame_analyses.description: Right-hand grasper (appears to be dominant hand based on control) approaching first peg on right side
Right grasper grasping first peg, securing with controlled grip
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Peg secured at mid-point, no fumbling

frame_analyses.description: First peg being transferred midair from right to left side of board
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth transfer motion, peg maintained in instrument

frame_analyses.description: Left-hand grasper receiving first peg from right-hand grasper at left peg position
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument exchange smooth, peg positioned on peg

frame_analyses.description: Second peg being picked up by right grasper from right side
Multiple pegs visible on left side of board; appears to show 4-5 pegs transferred to left side
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good pacing, systematic transfer of pegs

frame_analyses.description: Left-hand grasper picking up peg from left side as reversal begins (transfer back to right)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to reverse direction clean, proper instrument control

frame_analyses.description: Multiple pegs being transferred back to right side; 3-4 pegs visible on right side now
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Return transfer proceeding smoothly, maintained control

frame_analyses.description: Final peg placement appearing to occur on right side, pegs repositioned on right quadrant
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final pegs being seated, approach to completion

frame_analyses.description: All 6 pegs visible on right side of board in original peg positions; graspers withdrawn
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All pegs successfully placed back in starting positions, task completed

penalties.description: No dropped pegs, no pegs placed incorrectly, no incomplete placements observed

hand_dominance.notes: Clear demonstration of non-dominant to dominant and reverse transfer

confidence_rationale: Clear overhead camera view throughout task showing all pegs and their movements. Completion time clearly identifiable from initial peg pickup to final peg placement. No ambiguity regarding drops or misplacements. Only minor uncertainty in exact timestamps at phase boundaries, but overall task execution and timing highly visible.
cannot_determine: Exact frame-by-frame timestamps for each individual peg transfer (estimated from visible progression)
technique_summary: Competent intermediate-to-advanced performance. Task completed in 105 seconds, which falls in the intermediate range (60–120s = intermediate; 40–60s = expert). All 6 pegs successfully transferred bidirectionally with no drops or errors. Smooth instrument exchanges and maintained control throughout both forward and reverse directions.
strengths: No dropped pegs or misplacements throughout task
Smooth bidirectional transfer demonstrating proper technique
Good instrument control and grasp security
improvement_suggestions: Reduce completion time from 105s to under 80s by increasing peg pickup speed slightly
Optimize reverse transfer timing — currently slower than forward direction
Consider reducing hesitation at peg exchanges to approach expert time range (40–60s)
